mnemonics: Groups are VIPs: They must have (V)alid inverse for every element, (I)dentity element, and be (P)roperly closed under the operation.
keyPoints: A group is a set G with a binary operation that is closed, associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
Abelian groups are commutative groups where the operation is commutative (a * b = b * a) for all a, b in G.
Subgroups are subsets of a group that themselves form a group under the same operation.
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are commutative (Abelian); many important groups in cryptography and physics are non-commutative.
Forgetting to check closure when testing if a subset is a subgroup; a subset must be closed under the group operation.
Confusing the identity element; for example, in a group under multiplication, the identity is 1, not 0.
